name: Ruth Brown
abstract: Ruth Alston Brown (née Weston; January 12, 1928 – November 17, 2006) was an American singer-songwriter and actress, sometimes referred to as the "Queen of R&B". She was noted for bringing a pop music style to R&B music in a series of hit songs for Atlantic Records in the 1950s, such as "So Long", "Teardrops from My Eyes" and "(Mama) He T…

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: We worked together at the Apollo Theater. ... The singers – the male singer was Harry Belafonte. The female was Ruth Brown.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Ruth Brown was their feature artist, and I wrote this thing Tear Drops Keep Falling From My Eyes

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: He was on the same program with Ruth Brown. I found out that he could speak as well as he could perform on the instrument... When he introduced Ruth Brown, he said, 'Ladies and gentlemen, at this time it’s with a great deal of pleasure that I present to you the eminent and inimitable' ... Ruth Brown
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
